# Guitar Hero Academy — Copilot Instructions

## Project Context
Guitar Hero Academy is a Next.js 15 web app (App Router, static export) deployed on Vercel.
It runs entirely in the browser with no backend — all state lives in localStorage.
The app listens to the player's guitar through the microphone, detects pitch in real time
using a custom YIN algorithm, and scores each note as hit or miss against a scrolling tab rail.

The core gameplay loop:
  1. Player opens /practice/[levelId]
  2. Taps "Start Mic" → browser requests microphone permission
  3. 4-beat drum count-in plays before notes begin scrolling
  4. Notes scroll right-to-left; the playhead is the hit zone
  5. Pitch detection compares detected frequency to target — ±75 cents = hit
  6. Green = hit, dim red = miss (with actual note played or "silent" label)
  7. Level completes when all notes pass → results screen with score, stars, tips

There are 18 levels across 3 worlds:
  - World 1: Pentatonic & Blues Foundations (Em pentatonic, 5 CAGED positions, blues bends)
  - World 2: Major Scale & Modal Awareness (Dorian, Mixolydian, interval skips, sequencing)
  - World 3: Advanced Technique (Arpeggios, positional shifts, string-skipping, speed)

Key data shape: each Level has title, subtitle, description, bpm, difficulty (easy/medium/hard),
focus (technique goal), and notes[] (string, fret, targetFrequency, startBeat, duration).

Audio pipeline: getUserMedia → DynamicsCompressor → Gain (×4) → AnalyserNode → YIN (4096 samples
@ 44.1 kHz, sampled every 3rd rAF frame ~20 Hz). Hit window = note duration + 600 ms padding.
Release detection: player must go silent or shift ≥150 cents to retrigger.

Tab rail: rAF-driven, no React state per frame. Timing locked to AudioContext clock (not Date.now).
String colors: 1=blue, 2=amber, 3=green, 4=teal, 5=purple, 6=red.
Combo tints: ≥3=blue, ≥5=green, ≥10=gold.

Scoring: score = hits/total × 100. Stars: 3★ ≥80%, 2★ ≥60%, 1★ ≥40%, 0★ below.
Best stars per level are persisted and never overwritten by a worse run.

Drum synthesis: Web Audio API oscillators, no audio files. Kick/snare/hi-hat locked to BPM.
Drums play in Timed mode, silent in Practice mode.

All localStorage keys are prefixed gha-v1-:
  - gha-v1-progress        → completed level IDs + best star counts
  - gha-v1-achievements    → unlocked achievement IDs
  - gha-v1-session-history → last 80 sessions (capped)
  - gha-v1-setup-progress  → mic / tuner / audio check flags
  - gha-v1-practice-settings → mode, drum volume, timing offset ms
  - gha-anon-id            → UUID generated on first visit for future auth migration

All localStorage reads/writes are wrapped in try/catch — storage errors are silent.


## Tech Stack
- Next.js 15 (App Router, static export)
- TypeScript
- React hooks — no external state library
- Web Audio API — getUserMedia, AnalyserNode, DynamicsCompressor, oscillator synthesis
- Custom YIN pitch detection (de Cheveigné & Kawahara 2002)
- requestAnimationFrame loop for TabRail — no React state per frame
- localStorage — all persistence, no backend
- Inline styles + globals.css for keyframe animations and CSS variables
- Inter (body) + Playfair Display (display) via next/font
- Vercel deployment

## Coding Standards
- Use functional React components and custom hooks only — no class components.
- Keep all game timing locked to AudioContext clock, never Date.now or setInterval.
- Tab rail rendering must remain rAF-driven with no React setState per frame — mutate a ref or canvas directly.
- All localStorage access must be wrapped in try/catch; never let storage errors surface to the user.
- Best star counts are write-once-per-improvement — never overwrite with a lower value.
- Pitch detection runs every 3rd animation frame (~20 Hz); do not increase frequency without profiling.
- Drum synthesis uses Web Audio oscillators only — do not introduce audio file dependencies.
- Segment runs (front half, back half, last 8) must never overwrite full-level star progress.
- New levels added to a world must follow the existing Level shape in src/types/tab.ts.
- All 18 level pages are pre-rendered at build time via generateStaticParams — keep level IDs stable.
- OG image route must remain an edge function (not Node.js runtime) for fast cold starts on Vercel.
- Session history is capped at 80 entries — enforce this cap on every write.
- Anonymous ID (gha-anon-id) must never be reset or regenerated; it is the future auth migration key.
- Mobile styles live in the globals.css ≤680px media query layer — use className wiring, not inline style overrides, for responsive changes.
- Prefer inline styles for dynamic/computed values; prefer globals.css for animations and layout primitives.

## Notes
- No server, no account required — the app works entirely offline once loaded.
- AudioContext is created lazily on first user gesture to comply with browser autoplay policy.
- Tab rail uses a canvas or rAF ref pattern — avoid introducing React re-renders in the hot path.
- The cents meter doubles as a real-time tuner — keep it accurate to ±15 cents for the 'Perfect!' label.
- World completion ceremony fires only on the last level of a world; do not trigger mid-world.
- Daily challenge rotates deterministically by UTC date modulo 18 — keep the algorithm stable.
- Score sparkline shows last 8–12 timed runs only — exclude Practice mode runs from the chart.
- Per-string accuracy bars are shown only for strings actually used in the completed level.
- Scale diagram shows CAGED positions used in the level, colored by string — purely informational.
- 'Try faster' CTA appears only when score ≥80% and there is a higher BPM variant available.
- Auto-advance navigates to the next level only after a 3-star full-segment (not partial) run.
- Data export filename format: gha-export-YYYY-MM-DD.json
